# UC-22.59.2 · DPA + Ship Cyber Security Officer (CySO) + Alternate roster — 24x7 cyber-incident escalation cover with ISM-Code-grade availability monitoring

> **Criticality:** Critical &middot; **Difficulty:** Intermediate &middot; **Pillar:** Security &middot; **Type:** Availability, Governance &middot; **Wave:** Crawl &middot; **Status:** Verified

*When something goes wrong on a ship at any hour, there must always be someone ashore who can answer the phone and help. We watch the on-call schedule, ping each named person every 30 minutes, and ring the alarm if anyone goes missing or no-one is covering a shift.*

---

## Description

Surveils the DPA + CySO + Alternate on-call rota for continuous coverage and live heartbeat; raises a notable when the rota has a gap or any role's last heartbeat exceeds 60 minutes.

## Value

If a cyber incident occurs and no DPA / CySO is reachable, the Master's escalation path is broken, the flag-State reporting clock starts late, and the company is exposed to a major-non-conformity finding at the next DoC verification. This UC closes the gap before it becomes an audit observation.

## Implementation

(1) Seed `imo_dpa_cyso_roster_lookup` from the company's on-call platform (PagerDuty, Opsgenie, ServiceNow on-call). (2) Wire a heartbeat ping every 30 minutes from each on-call role's primary device (mobile app push-acknowledgement, sat-phone keep-alive). (3) Schedule UC-22.59.2 every 15 minutes; route any `gap_indicator=true` to the CISO + Head of Fleet Operations at severity P1.

## Evidence

Saved search `UC-22.59.2` archived daily to `audit_evidence` with `imo_clause="IMO-MSC-FAL-Circ-3-s2-4"`. Every row pins (role, person_name, vessel_or_fleet_scope, on_call_window, contact_channels, last_heartbeat, heartbeat_age_minutes, gap_indicator).

## Control test

### Positive scenario

Allow a gap in the DPA on-call rota where no Alternate is named for a 6-hour window. UC-22.59.2 MUST raise a `gap_indicator=true` notable within 30 minutes of the gap window opening.

### Negative scenario

Maintain DPA + Alternate continuous cover. UC-22.59.2 MUST record `gap_indicator=false` and `heartbeat_age_minutes < 60` for every role-row.

## Detailed Implementation

## Prerequisites

- Splunk Enterprise >= 9.2 or Splunk Cloud.
- An on-call platform integrated via Splunk Add-on for PagerDuty / Opsgenie / ServiceNow.
- A heartbeat-ping mechanism from each on-call device (push notification ack, sat-phone keep-alive).

## Step 1 — Roster KV Store

```ini
[imo_dpa_cyso_roster_lookup]
enforceTypes = true
field.role = string
field.person_name = string
field.vessel_or_fleet_scope = string
field.on_call_start = time
field.on_call_end = time
field.primary_phone = string
field.secondary_phone = string
field.sat_phone = string
field.satcom_email = string
field.last_heartbeat = time
field.heartbeat_source = string
```

## Step 2 — Heartbeat wiring

Each on-call device must publish a heartbeat through the on-call app (PagerDuty mobile app push-acknowledgement, Opsgenie heartbeat ping) or a custom HEC event. The Splunk Add-on writes the latest heartbeat to the KV Store via `outputlookup`.

## Step 3 — Schedule

```ini
[UC-22.59.2: IMO DPA + CySO Roster Health]
cron_schedule = */15 * * * *
actions = summary_index, notable
action.summary_index._name = audit_evidence
action.summary_index.marker = uc=22.59.2,reg=IMO-MSC-428-98,clause=IMO-MSC-FAL-Circ-3-s2-4
alert.severity = 5
```

## Step 4 — Runbooks

**`gap_indicator=true`** (owner: CISO + Head of Fleet Ops, severity P1): Within 30 minutes, restore coverage; within 24 hours, write a corrective-action note to the SMS audit log; at the next monthly governance meeting, present root cause.

## Step 5 — Troubleshooting

- **Sat-phone latency**: a vessel in deep-ocean satcom edge may take 60+ minutes for a heartbeat; tune per-vessel tolerance based on `last_known_position` and the company's satcom coverage map.
- **Vessel without sat-phone**: rare but possible for inland-only operations; use VHF radio and a fall-back contact rather than the cyber roster.

## SPL

```spl
| inputlookup imo_dpa_cyso_roster_lookup
| eval now_ts = now()
| eval heartbeat_age_minutes = round((now_ts - last_heartbeat) / 60, 1)
| eval in_window = if(now_ts >= on_call_start AND now_ts <= on_call_end, "true", "false")
| stats values(person_name) as people, values(in_window) as in_window_vals, max(last_heartbeat) as latest_heartbeat by role, vessel_or_fleet_scope
| eval heartbeat_age_minutes = round((now() - latest_heartbeat) / 60, 1)
| eval covered = if(mvfind(in_window_vals, "true") >= 0, "true", "false")
| eval gap_indicator = case(covered = "false", "true", heartbeat_age_minutes > 60, "true", 1=1, "false")
| where gap_indicator = "true"
| table role vessel_or_fleet_scope people latest_heartbeat heartbeat_age_minutes covered gap_indicator
```

## Visualization

(Panel 1) Live coverage matrix (role x time-of-day) for the next 24 hours. (Panel 2) Heartbeat age distribution across all roles. (Panel 3) Historical gaps over the last 30 days. (Panel 4) On-call hand-over compliance score.

## Known False Positives

1. **Hand-over micro-gap**: a 5-minute hand-over window between outgoing and incoming on-call is normal; ignore gaps under 10 minutes. 2. **Sat-phone heartbeat delay**: vessels in poor satcom coverage may legitimately have a delayed heartbeat; pin tolerance to 90 minutes for vessels in `vessel_status=offshore` or `last_known_position` in known coverage holes. 3. **Public-holiday short-staffing**: pre-approved short-staff windows during major holidays are accepted via a holiday register.

**Suppression mechanism**: KV Store `imo_22_59_2_exceptions` with the CISO's documented approval and a written justification.

## References

- [IMO Resolution MSC.428(98)](https://wwwcdn.imo.org/localresources/en/OurWork/Security/Documents/Resolution%20MSC.428(98).pdf)
- [IMO MSC-FAL.1/Circ.3 Rev.2 — Guidelines on maritime cyber risk management](https://wwwcdn.imo.org/localresources/en/OurWork/Security/Documents/MSC-FAL.1-Circ.3-Rev.2.pdf)
- [BIMCO Guidelines on Cyber Security Onboard Ships](https://www.bimco.org/about-us-and-our-members/publications/the-guidelines-on-cyber-security-onboard-ships)
